fr.recommendations: Improving clear access points around the main building and addressing limited rear access might support quicker response times and enhance accessibility for emergency personnel. Managing and reducing dense vegetation, particularly in areas surrounding the pool and open green space, could facilitate improved visibility and unobstructed movement for responders. Designating specific pathways or access routes through the landscaped areas may assist emergency units in navigating the property more effectively. Ensuring that emergency responders can easily identify and access the separate structures like the detached building and tennis court may streamline operational coordination. Overall, strategic planning of clear pathways and routine vegetation management might enhance emergency operations and facilitate efficient staging and movement across the property.

wildfire.recommendations: Improving vegetation management adjacent to the structure by increasing separation and reducing dense tree canopy near the building may enhance defensible space. Creating breaks in the tree canopy and managing scattered trees can reduce fuel continuity and create a less continuous pathway for fire. The open terrain around the property presents an opportunity to expand fuel breaks further, potentially increasing the effectiveness of defensible space. The existing tennis court and water feature should be maintained as part of the property’s strategic fuel modification efforts, as these features generally do not contribute to fuel loads and can help limit fire spread. Regular maintenance of the vegetated drainage is advisable to ensure it doesn't become a source of embers. Within the cluster of structures, proper management of vegetation can help reduce exposure, while preserving surrounding open space can continue to improve overall property resilience by acting as a natural barrier and supporting wildfire preparedness.
